# Style Reference Guide

<!-- Generated by design-style-agent -->
<!-- Feature: Contact Form with Admin Inbox -->

This guide covers styling decisions that CSS custom properties alone cannot
express. The developer agent should follow these conventions when implementing
the contact form and admin inbox components.

---

## Typography

**Heading hierarchy**

- `h1` — Page title only (e.g., "Contact" as the page heading). Use Shadcn's
  `className="text-3xl font-bold tracking-tight"` pattern. One per page.
- `h2` — Section headings within a page (e.g., "Inbox" in the admin view).
  Use `text-xl font-semibold`.
- `h3` — Card-level or group headings if needed (e.g., per-message sender
  name in the admin inbox). Use `text-base font-semibold`.
- No heading levels should be skipped for accessibility.

**Body text**

- Default body: `text-sm` (0.875rem) inside form cards and inbox rows.
- Long message body preview: `text-sm text-muted-foreground` with
  `whitespace-pre-wrap` to preserve line breaks from the visitor's message.

**Labels and captions**

- Form field labels: `text-sm font-medium` — use Shadcn `<Label />` component,
  not plain `<label>` HTML.
- Inline validation errors: `text-sm text-destructive` with
  `role="alert" aria-live="polite"` on the wrapping element.
- Character counter (Message field): `text-xs text-muted-foreground`. When
  at or above 1000 characters, switch to `text-xs text-destructive font-medium`.
- Timestamps in admin inbox: `text-xs text-muted-foreground`.

**Font stack**

- Geist Sans (already mapped to `--font-sans` in globals.css). Do not specify
  a different font family.

---

## Spacing

**Scale preference**

Tailwind's default 4px base unit applies. Use multiples of 4px.

**Contact form card**

- Card outer padding: `p-6` (24px) on medium+ screens, `p-4` (16px) on mobile.
- Field vertical gap: `space-y-4` between form field groups.
- Label-to-input gap: handled by Shadcn `<FormItem />` defaults.
- Submit button: full-width `w-full`, `mt-2` top margin from the last field.

**Success and error banners**

- Inline success message: same card container as the form, `p-6`, centred text.
- Inline error banner at form top: `p-3 rounded-md bg-destructive/10
  text-destructive text-sm`, `mb-4` gap before the first field.

**Admin inbox**

- Inbox list: `space-y-3` between message cards.
- Message card body: `p-4`.
- Password gate: centred card, max-width `max-w-sm`, with `p-6` padding.

---

## Component Selection

**Contact form container**

- Wrap the form in a Shadcn `<Card />` with `<CardHeader />` and
  `<CardContent />`. The card is the embeddable unit (R1).
- Do not use a plain `<div>` — the card provides consistent background, border,
  and shadow in both light and dark themes.

**Form fields**

- Text inputs (Name, Email): Shadcn `<Input />` inside Shadcn `<FormItem />` /
  `<FormControl />` / `<FormMessage />` (React Hook Form integration).
- Subject dropdown: Shadcn `<Select />` with `<SelectTrigger />`,
  `<SelectContent />`, `<SelectItem />` — not a native `<select>`. This ensures
  consistent styling and accessibility across browsers.
- Message textarea: Shadcn `<Textarea />` with `rows={5}`.
- Honeypot field: a plain `<input type="text" tabIndex={-1} aria-hidden="true"
  className="hidden" />` — hidden from view and screen readers (R13).

**Submit button**

- Shadcn `<Button variant="default" size="default" type="submit">`. Uses the
  `--primary` token for background, so it reflects the brand color automatically.
- During submission: add `disabled` + a spinner via Lucide `<Loader2 />` with
  `className="animate-spin"` inside the button (R8).
- At the 1000-character message limit: `disabled` on both the button and add
  visual styling change on the counter (BR5).

**Success state**

- Replace the `<CardContent />` (not the full card) with the success message
  markup. Keep the `<CardHeader />` visible.
- Include a Shadcn `<Button variant="ghost" size="sm">` for "Send another
  message" (R10).
- Use `tabIndex={-1}` with `ref` + `focus()` on the success message container
  after transition (R9 / NFR2).

**API error banner**

- Not a Shadcn `<Alert />` component — use a plain styled `<div>` with
  `role="alert"` and `tabIndex={-1}` so focus can be programmatically moved
  to it (R11 / NFR2).
- Style: `bg-destructive/10 border border-destructive/20 text-destructive
  rounded-md p-3 text-sm`.

**Admin inbox**

- Password gate: a standalone `<Card className="max-w-sm mx-auto mt-16" />`.
- Inbox messages: each message rendered as a Shadcn `<Card />`. Do not use a
  `<Table />` — the message body is multi-line text; a card list is more
  readable on mobile (NFR1).
- No `<Dialog />`, `<Drawer />`, or tabs — messages are shown directly inline.

---

## Layout

**Contact form page**

- The form component is embeddable (R1). It should have no built-in page margins.
- When placed on a standalone page, the host page centres it: `max-w-lg mx-auto
  px-4 py-12`.
- Single-column at all viewport sizes (NFR1). No grid layout inside the form.

**Admin inbox page**

- Max content width: `max-w-2xl mx-auto px-4 py-8`.
- Password gate is full-page centred (see Component Selection above).
- Inbox header: an `h1` with the inbox title and a message count badge
  (`<Badge variant="secondary">`).
- Message list: stacked vertically, no sidebar, no columns.

**Responsive behavior**

- The form card: full-width on mobile (no horizontal padding on the card itself;
  padding comes from the host page wrapper). On screens `sm:` and above the
  card padding increases via the `p-6` class.
- Font sizes and spacing remain constant across breakpoints — do not scale up
  heading sizes on large screens for this feature.

---

## Motion and Animation

**Transition philosophy**

Subtle and purposeful — this is a utility form, not a marketing page.

**Form-to-success transition**

- Fade in the success message: `transition-opacity duration-200 ease-in`.
- Do not animate the card dimensions. Simply replace the inner content and let
  the card height adjust naturally.

**Error banner appearance**

- Fade in: same `transition-opacity duration-150 ease-in`.
- No slide or scale — keep it simple and non-distracting.

**Button loading state**

- Spinner only (`<Loader2 className="animate-spin" />`). No transition on the
  button itself — just swap the label text for a spinner and disable immediately.

**Reduced motion**

- `globals.css` already includes `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce)`
  that sets `animation-duration: 0.01ms` and `transition-duration: 0.01ms`
  globally. No additional reduced-motion handling is needed in component code.
- Do not manually check `prefers-reduced-motion` in JavaScript.

---

## Accessibility

**Minimum contrast**

- All text/background pairs must meet WCAG 2.1 AA: 4.5:1 for normal text,
  3:1 for large text (18px+) and UI components.
- Design tokens have been verified against this standard (see contrast notes
  in design-tokens.css).
- Do not override `--destructive`, `--muted-foreground`, or `--ring` with
  custom inline styles that could reduce contrast.

**Focus rings**

- Do not use `outline-none` or `focus:outline-none` on interactive elements
  unless you replace the focus indicator with a custom one using `ring-2
  ring-ring ring-offset-2`.
- Shadcn components provide built-in focus rings via the `--ring` token.
  Confirm the ring is visible before shipping (NFR2).

**Labels and ARIA**

- Every form field must have a visible `<Label />` linked via `htmlFor` /
  `id`. Do not rely on `placeholder` as the only label.
- The honeypot field must have `aria-hidden="true"` and `tabIndex={-1}`.
- The success message container: `role="status"` and programmatic focus after
  render (R9).
- The error banner: `role="alert"` and programmatic focus after render (R11).
- The character counter: expose via `aria-describedby` on the textarea so
  screen readers announce the count.

**Keyboard navigation**

- Tab order must follow the visual top-to-bottom order of the form fields.
- The Shadcn `<Select />` for Subject must be keyboard-operable (it is by
  default — do not suppress native keyboard behaviour).
- The "Send another message" button must be reachable and activatable via
  keyboard.

---

## Brand Tone

**Overall voice**

Professional but approachable — this is a personal portfolio. The language
should feel human and direct, not corporate.

**Form labels and placeholders**

- Labels: short and descriptive ("Your name", "Email address", "Subject",
  "Message").
- Placeholders: optional, light guidance only — not a substitute for labels.
  Example: `placeholder="What's on your mind?"` for the message field.
- Do not use all-caps labels.

**Validation errors**

- Use the exact error strings defined in the FRS business rules (BR1–BR4).
  Do not paraphrase them:
  - "Name must be at least 2 characters."
  - "Please enter a valid email address."
  - "Please select a subject."
  - "Message must be at least 10 characters."
- Errors are factual and brief — no apologetic language ("Oops!", "Sorry!").

**Success message**

- Heading: "Message sent!" (concise, confirmation-oriented).
- Body: "Thanks for reaching out — I'll get back to you soon." (warm, personal).
- "Send another message" link/button: lowercase, understated.

**API error message**

- "Something went wrong. Please try again." — honest, non-technical, no blame.

**Admin inbox empty state**

- "No messages yet." — simple. No illustration needed for initial scope.

**Admin password gate**

- Label: "Admin password".
- Error on wrong password: "Incorrect password. Please try again."
- Submit button: "Unlock inbox".

**Button labels**

- Submit: "Send message" (not "Submit").
- Loading state: "Sending..." (not "Loading..." or "Please wait...").
- "Send another message" after success.
